message:
br-bd-caex2 implement btrfs_ino_lookup back-reference walk for non-root objectid

`BTRFS_IOC_INO_LOOKUP` previously only resolved the subvolume root
(objectid 256); any other objectid fell through to a generic ENOENT
with the inline comment 'Full back-reference walking not implemented
yet.' This contradicted bd-jfsga's 'done' closure whose acceptance
criterion was 'returns correct path for known inodes.'

Fix: walk `INODE_REF` items from the target objectid up to the
subvolume root, prepending each (parent_oid, name) pair. Output
matches the Linux `fs/btrfs/ioctl.c::btrfs_search_path_in_tree`
format: `/`-separated relative path with trailing `/` before the
terminating NUL.

Scope:
- Cross-subvolume resolution (ROOT_REF) out of scope for V1; non-
  mounted tree ids now surface `UnsupportedFeature` with a clear
  message rather than silent ENOENT.
- When the target has multiple back-refs (hard links), the first
  encountered in fs-tree order wins — deterministic, matches kernel
  behavior for typical lookups.
- Defense-in-depth: cycle detection via visited set, 4096-hop
  depth cap to bound walks over corrupt trees.

Algorithm extracted into pure helper `btrfs_build_path_from_inode_refs(&entries, objectid)`
so it's unit-testable without constructing a full btrfs image. Six
new tests cover: subvolume root, single hop, multi-hop `a/b/c/`,
missing back-ref → NotFound, cycle → Corruption, hard-link
first-wins determinism.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
